frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Bro honestly, Indian wear mein girls look much more decent and graceful. Office mein bhi jab someone dresses simple and traditional, automatically a better impression padta hai — aajkal these ultra-modern outfits just invite unnecessary attention.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links women's clothing to decency and respectability, implying traditional dress signals better character. It also subtly blames harassment or scrutiny on 'ultra-modern outfits' by saying they 'invite unnecessary attention.'
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ankit. Age 29. A software developer in Pune. You are in a one on one WhatsApp chat with your closest male colleague Saurabh. Your female team member Riya has an important client call today but your manager just told you to reassign it because Riya called in sick again due to Ôwoman-relatedÕ issue. You are telling Saurabh why you think this keeps happening with female colleagues.
